briefdescription.para: Return true if we are currently handling a system call from user mode.

detaileddescription.para: Inside z_vrfy functions, we always know that we are handling a system call invoked from user context.
However, some checks that are only relevant to user mode must instead be placed deeper within the implementation. This API is useful to conditionally make these checks.
For performance reasons, whenever possible, checks should be placed in the relevant z_vrfy function since these are completely skipped when a syscall is invoked.
This will return true only if we are handling a syscall for a user thread. If the system call was invoked from supervisor mode, or we are not handling a system call, this will return false.

simplesect.para: This is an internal API. Do not use unless you are extending functionality in the Zephyr tree.
whether the current context is handling a syscall for a user mode thread

memberdef.argsstring: (struct k_object *ko, enum k_objects otype, enum _obj_init_check init)
memberdef.name: k_object_validate

briefdescription.para: Ensure a system object is a valid object of the expected type.

detaileddescription.para: Searches for the object and ensures that it is indeed an object of the expected type, that the caller has the right permissions on it, and that the object has been initialized.
This function is intended to be called on the kernel-side system call handlers to validate kernel object pointers passed in from userspace.

parameterdescription.para: Kernel object metadata pointer, or NULL
Expected type of the kernel object, or K_OBJ_ANY if type doesn't matter
Indicate whether the object needs to already be in initialized or uninitialized state, or that we don't care

simplesect.para: This is an internal API. Do not use unless you are extending functionality in the Zephyr tree.
0 If the object is valid -EBADF if not a valid object of the specified type -EPERM If the caller does not have permissions -EINVAL Object is not initialized

memberdef.argsstring: (int retval, const void *obj, struct k_object *ko, enum k_objects otype)
memberdef.name: k_object_dump_error

briefdescription.para: Dump out error information on failed k_object_validate() call.

parameterdescription.para: Kernel object we were trying to verify
If retval=-EPERM, struct k_object * that was looked up, or NULL
Expected type of the kernel object

simplesect.para: This is an internal API. Do not use unless you are extending functionality in the Zephyr tree.

briefdescription.para: Kernel object validation function.

detaileddescription.para: Retrieve metadata for a kernel object. This function is implemented in the gperf script footer, see gen_kobject_list.py

parameterdescription.para: Address of kernel object to get metadata

simplesect.para: Kernel object's metadata, or NULL if the parameter wasn't the memory address of a kernel object
This is an internal API. Do not use unless you are extending functionality in the Zephyr tree.

briefdescription.para: Iterate over all the kernel object metadata in the system.

parameterdescription.para: function to run on each struct k_object
Context pointer to pass to each invocation

simplesect.para: This is an internal API. Do not use unless you are extending functionality in the Zephyr tree.

briefdescription.para: Copy all kernel object permissions from the parent to the child.

parameterdescription.para: Parent thread, to get permissions from
Child thread, to copy permissions to

simplesect.para: This is an internal API. Do not use unless you are extending functionality in the Zephyr tree.

briefdescription.para: Grant a thread permission to a kernel object.

simplesect.para: This is an internal API. Do not use unless you are extending functionality in the Zephyr tree.

briefdescription.para: Revoke a thread's permission to a kernel object.

simplesect.para: This is an internal API. Do not use unless you are extending functionality in the Zephyr tree.

briefdescription.para: Revoke access to all objects for the provided thread.

simplesect.para: Unlike k_thread_perms_clear(), this function will not clear permissions on public objects.
This is an internal API. Do not use unless you are extending functionality in the Zephyr tree.

briefdescription.para: Clear initialization state of a kernel object.

detaileddescription.para: Intended for thread objects upon thread exit, or for other kernel objects that were released back to an object pool.

simplesect.para: This is an internal API. Do not use unless you are extending functionality in the Zephyr tree.

briefdescription.para: Initialize and reset permissions to only access by the caller.

detaileddescription.para: Intended for scenarios where objects are fetched from slab pools and may have had different permissions set during prior usage.

This is only intended for pools of objects, where such objects are acquired and released to the pool. If an object has already been used, we do not want stale permission information hanging around, the object should only have permissions on the caller. Objects which are not managed by a pool-like mechanism should not use this API.
The object will be marked as initialized and the calling thread granted access to it.

simplesect.para: This is an internal API. Do not use unless you are extending functionality in the Zephyr tree.

briefdescription.para: Obtain the size of a C string passed from user mode.

detaileddescription.para: Given a C string pointer and a maximum size, obtain the true size of the string (not including the trailing NULL byte) just as if calling strnlen() on it, with the same semantics of strnlen() with respect to the return value and the maxlen parameter.
Any memory protection faults triggered by the examination of the string will be safely handled and an error code returned.
NOTE: Doesn't guarantee that user mode has actual access to this string, you will need to still do a K_SYSCALL_MEMORY_READ() with the obtained size value to guarantee this.

parameterdescription.para: Maximum number of characters to examine
Pointer to int, filled in with -1 on memory error, 0 on success

simplesect.para: undefined on error, or strlen(src) if that is less than maxlen, or maxlen if there were no NULL terminating characters within the first maxlen bytes.
This is an internal API. Do not use unless you are extending functionality in the Zephyr tree.

briefdescription.para: Copy data from userspace into a resource pool allocation.

detaileddescription.para: Given a pointer and a size, allocate a similarly sized buffer in the caller's resource pool and copy all the data within it to the newly allocated buffer. This will need to be freed later with k_free().
Checks are done to ensure that the current thread would have read access to the provided buffer.

simplesect.para: An allocated buffer with the data copied within it, or NULL if some error condition occurred
This is an internal API. Do not use unless you are extending functionality in the Zephyr tree.

briefdescription.para: Copy data from user mode.

detaileddescription.para: Given a userspace pointer and a size, copies data from it into a provided destination buffer, performing checks to ensure that the caller would have appropriate access when in user mode.

simplesect.para: This is an internal API. Do not use unless you are extending functionality in the Zephyr tree.

briefdescription.para: Copy data to user mode.

detaileddescription.para: Given a userspace pointer and a size, copies data to it from a provided source buffer, performing checks to ensure that the caller would have appropriate access when in user mode.

simplesect.para: This is an internal API. Do not use unless you are extending functionality in the Zephyr tree.

briefdescription.para: Copy a C string from userspace into a resource pool allocation.

detaileddescription.para: Given a C string and maximum length, duplicate the string using an allocation from the calling thread's resource pool. This will need to be freed later with k_free().
Checks are performed to ensure that the string is valid memory and that the caller has access to it in user mode.

parameterdescription.para: Maximum size of the string including trailing NULL

simplesect.para: The duplicated string, or NULL if an error occurred.
This is an internal API. Do not use unless you are extending functionality in the Zephyr tree.

briefdescription.para: Copy a C string from userspace into a provided buffer.

detaileddescription.para: Given a C string and maximum length, copy the string into a buffer.
Checks are performed to ensure that the string is valid memory and that the caller has access to it in user mode.

parameterdescription.para: Maximum size of the string including trailing NULL
if the source string is too long with respect to maxlen

simplesect.para: This is an internal API. Do not use unless you are extending functionality in the Zephyr tree.

memberdef.argsstring: (struct k_object *ko, const void *obj, enum k_objects otype, enum _obj_init_check init)
memberdef.name: k_object_validation_check

briefdescription.para: Induce a kernel oops.

detaileddescription.para: This macro can be used to induce a kernel oops which will kill the calling thread.

simplesect.para: This is an internal API. Do not use unless you are extending functionality in the Zephyr tree.

memberdef.name: K_SYSCALL_VERIFY_MSG
memberdef.initializer: ({ \ bool expr_copy = !(expr); \ if (expr_copy) { \ TOOLCHAIN_DISABLE_WARNING(TOOLCHAIN_WARNING_SHADOW) \ LOG_MODULE_DECLARE(os, CONFIG_KERNEL_LOG_LEVEL); \ TOOLCHAIN_ENABLE_WARNING(TOOLCHAIN_WARNING_SHADOW) \ LOG_ERR("syscall %s failed check: " fmt, \ __func__, ##__VA_ARGS__); \ } \ expr_copy; })

briefdescription.para: Runtime expression check for system call arguments.

detaileddescription.para: Used in handler functions to perform various runtime checks on arguments, and generate a kernel oops if anything is not expected, printing a custom message.

parameterdescription.para: Boolean expression to verify, a false result will trigger an oops
Printf-style format string (followed by appropriate variadic arguments) to print on verification failure

simplesect.para: False on success, True on failure
This is an internal API. Do not use unless you are extending functionality in the Zephyr tree.

briefdescription.para: Runtime expression check for system call arguments.

detaileddescription.para: Used in handler functions to perform various runtime checks on arguments, and generate a kernel oops if anything is not expected.

parameterdescription.para: Boolean expression to verify, a false result will trigger an oops. A stringified version of this expression will be printed.

simplesect.para: 0 on success, nonzero on failure
This is an internal API. Do not use unless you are extending functionality in the Zephyr tree.

briefdescription.para: Macro to check if size is negative.

detaileddescription.para: K_SYSCALL_MEMORY can be called with signed/unsigned types and because of that if we check if size is greater or equal to zero, many static analyzers complain about no effect expression.

simplesect.para: true if size is valid, false otherwise
This is an internal API. Do not use unless you are extending functionality in the Zephyr tree.

briefdescription.para: Runtime check that a user thread has read and/or write permission to a memory area.

detaileddescription.para: Checks that the particular memory area is readable and/or writeable by the currently running thread if the CPU was in user mode, and generates a kernel oops if it wasn't. Prevents userspace from getting the kernel to read and/or modify memory the thread does not have access to, or passing in garbage pointers that would crash/pagefault the kernel if dereferenced.

parameterdescription.para: If the thread should be able to write to this memory, not just read it

simplesect.para: 0 on success, nonzero on failure
This is an internal API. Do not use unless you are extending functionality in the Zephyr tree.

briefdescription.para: Runtime check that a user thread has read permission to a memory area.

detaileddescription.para: Checks that the particular memory area is readable by the currently running thread if the CPU was in user mode, and generates a kernel oops if it wasn't. Prevents userspace from getting the kernel to read memory the thread does not have access to, or passing in garbage pointers that would crash/pagefault the kernel if dereferenced.

simplesect.para: 0 on success, nonzero on failure
This is an internal API. Do not use unless you are extending functionality in the Zephyr tree.

briefdescription.para: Runtime check that a user thread has write permission to a memory area.

detaileddescription.para: Checks that the particular memory area is readable and writable by the currently running thread if the CPU was in user mode, and generates a kernel oops if it wasn't. Prevents userspace from getting the kernel to read or modify memory the thread does not have access to, or passing in garbage pointers that would crash/pagefault the kernel if dereferenced.

simplesect.para: 0 on success, nonzero on failure
This is an internal API. Do not use unless you are extending functionality in the Zephyr tree.

memberdef.name: K_SYSCALL_MEMORY_ARRAY
memberdef.initializer: ({ \ size_t product; \ K_SYSCALL_VERIFY_MSG(!size_mul_overflow((size_t)(nmemb), \ (size_t)(size), \ &product), \ "%zux%zu array is too large", \ (size_t)(nmemb), (size_t)(size)) || \ K_SYSCALL_MEMORY(ptr, product, write); \ })

briefdescription.para: Validate user thread has read permission for sized array.

detaileddescription.para: Used when the memory region is expressed in terms of number of elements and each element size, handles any overflow issues with computing the total array bounds. Otherwise see _SYSCALL_MEMORY_READ.

parameterdescription.para: Number of elements in the array

simplesect.para: 0 on success, nonzero on failure
This is an internal API. Do not use unless you are extending functionality in the Zephyr tree.

briefdescription.para: Validate user thread has read/write permission for sized array.

detaileddescription.para: Used when the memory region is expressed in terms of number of elements and each element size, handles any overflow issues with computing the total array bounds. Otherwise see _SYSCALL_MEMORY_WRITE.

parameterdescription.para: Number of elements in the array

simplesect.para: 0 on success, nonzero on failure
This is an internal API. Do not use unless you are extending functionality in the Zephyr tree.

memberdef.name: K_SYSCALL_DRIVER_OP
memberdef.initializer: ({ \ struct api_name *__device__ = (struct api_name *) \ ((const struct device *)(ptr))->api; \ K_SYSCALL_VERIFY_MSG(__device__->op != NULL, \ "Operation %s not defined for driver " \ "instance %p", \ # op, __device__); \ })

briefdescription.para: Runtime check driver object pointer for presence of operation.

detaileddescription.para: Validates if the driver object is capable of performing a certain operation.

parameterdescription.para: Name of the driver API struct (e.g. gpio_driver_api)

simplesect.para: 0 on success, nonzero on failure
This is an internal API. Do not use unless you are extending functionality in the Zephyr tree.

memberdef.name: K_SYSCALL_SPECIFIC_DRIVER
memberdef.initializer: ({ \ const struct device *_dev = (const struct device *)_device; \ K_SYSCALL_OBJ(_dev, _dtype) || \ K_SYSCALL_VERIFY_MSG(_dev->api == _api, \ "API structure mismatch"); \ })

briefdescription.para: Runtime check that device object is of a specific driver type.

detaileddescription.para: Checks that the driver object passed in is initialized, the caller has correct permissions, and that it belongs to the specified driver subsystems. Additionally, all devices store a structure pointer of the driver's API. If this doesn't match the value provided, the check will fail.

This provides an easy way to determine if a device object not only belongs to a particular subsystem, but is of a specific device driver implementation. Useful for defining out-of-subsystem system calls which are implemented for only one driver.

parameterdescription.para: Expected kernel object type for the provided device pointer
Expected driver API structure memory address

simplesect.para: 0 on success, nonzero on failure
This is an internal API. Do not use unless you are extending functionality in the Zephyr tree.

briefdescription.para: Runtime check kernel object pointer for non-init functions.

detaileddescription.para: Calls k_object_validate and triggers a kernel oops if the check fails. For use in system call handlers which are not init functions; a fatal error will occur if the object is not initialized.

simplesect.para: 0 on success, nonzero on failure
This is an internal API. Do not use unless you are extending functionality in the Zephyr tree.

briefdescription.para: Runtime check kernel object pointer for non-init functions.

detaileddescription.para: See description of _SYSCALL_IS_OBJ. No initialization checks are done. Intended for init functions where objects may be re-initialized at will.

simplesect.para: 0 on success, nonzero on failure
This is an internal API. Do not use unless you are extending functionality in the Zephyr tree.

briefdescription.para: Runtime check kernel object pointer for non-init functions.

detaileddescription.para: See description of _SYSCALL_IS_OBJ. Triggers a fatal error if the object is initialized. Intended for init functions where objects, once initialized, can only be re-used when their initialization state expires due to some other mechanism.

simplesect.para: 0 on success, nonzero on failure
This is an internal API. Do not use unless you are extending functionality in the Zephyr tree.

briefdescription.para: User mode and Syscall APIs.
